elements.title: Sirius B
elements.contents: ... that the star Sirius B is slightly smaller than Earth (comparison pictured)?

elements.title: the father of Lithuanian scientific forestry
elements.contents: ... that the father of Lithuanian scientific forestry also directed theatre, organized a school, compiled maps, and wrote a dictionary of plants, a mathematics textbook, and a collection of poetry?

elements.title: Haibane Renmei
elements.contents: ... that the screenwriter of Haibane Renmei was given two days to finish the script for the finale?

elements.title: Lisa Fagin Davis
elements.contents: ... that Lisa Fagin Davis's analysis of handwriting differences in the Voynich manuscript concluded that it was likely the work of five scribes?

elements.title: Flamboyant
elements.contents: ... that the Dorian Electra album Flamboyant is named both as a reference to a style of Gothic architecture and to reclaim a word historically used to shame gay men?

elements.title: Jimmy Lawrence
elements.contents: ... that Jimmy Lawrence, the fifth player selected in the first NFL draft, was reported to have been killed in action no less than three times?

elements.title: 2003 São Tomé and Príncipe coup d'état
elements.contents: ... that one of the leaders of the 2003 São Tomé and Príncipe coup d'état also participated in a failed coup attempt in 1988?

elements.title: Chris Appelhans
elements.contents: ... that Chris Appelhans spent "six to eight years in the wilderness learning how to write" before directing his first film?

elements.title: Chess World Cup 1988–1989
elements.contents: ... that the Chess World Cup 1988–1989 featured a game of human chess between Anatoly Karpov and Jan Timman?
